clean_story:
Now I will teach you today.

Sun rises, but cold still bites like wolf’s breath. Sky stays pale, not bright like fire-season—soft, like wet hide stretched thin. Shadows crawl slow, not sharp like spear-tip at noon, but dull, like old bone in mud. Wind carries no scent of rain or beast, just empty air, like cave when all leave to hunt. Your breath fogs like steam over hot meat, but meat is gone—only hard ground and bare branches scratch like claw-marks. This is the time between big cold and new green, when world holds still—today.
